title: Brain Anatomy
subtitle: Meet the four structures every neuro learner should recognise.

cards.title: Cerebrum
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure forms the largest part of the brain?
backPoints: The largest part of the brain.
Supports conscious thought, memory and language.
Processes sensation and controls voluntary movement.
cards.memoryLine: Think: awareness, thought, sensation and movement.

cards.title: Cerebellum
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure helps make movement smooth and coordinated?
backPoints: Cerebellar injury can cause ataxia and poor coordination.

cards.title: Brainstem
cards.frontPrompt: Which structure connects the brain to the spinal cord?
backPoints: Made up of the midbrain, pons and medulla.
Carries pathways between the brain and spinal cord.
Supports consciousness, breathing and cardiovascular control.
Many cranial nerve nuclei are located here.

cards.title: Ventricles
cards.frontPrompt: Which spaces contain and circulate cerebrospinal fluid?
backPoints: Includes the two lateral ventricles, third ventricle and fourth ventricle.
CSF is mainly produced by the choroid plexus.
Obstruction to CSF flow can cause hydrocephalus.
cards.memoryLine: Think: CSF production, circulation and protection.

questions.question: Which structure is responsible for conscious thought, sensation and voluntary movement?
questions.feedback: The cerebrum supports higher cognitive function, sensation and voluntary movement.

questions.question: A patient has marked ataxia and poor coordination. Which structure may be affected?
questions.feedback: The cerebellum coordinates balance and smooth, accurate movement.

questions.question: Which three parts make up the brainstem?
questions.feedback: The brainstem consists of the midbrain, pons and medulla.

questions.question: Where is cerebrospinal fluid found?
questions.feedback: CSF circulates through the ventricles and the subarachnoid space.

questions.question: Obstruction of CSF flow can lead to:
questions.feedback: Blocked CSF circulation can enlarge the ventricles and cause hydrocephalus.
